Sample enclosed in aluminum holder hung vertically in
5.08-cm thick, for runs with 11-MeV incident neutrons.
24-MeV incident neutron runs used a 29.2-cm thick,
18.4-cm diameter NE-224 liquid scintillator. Both are
shielded from magnetic fields with layers of mu-metal.
Flux monitor is a bubble free NE-224 liquid
6.6-meter flight path used. Main detectors placed
inside water tank to insure radiation shielding. Front
of water tank shielded by iron and paraffin and used
a copper shadow bar. Computer code PKFIT used to
extract peak areas from time of flight spectra.
Dead time, source anisotropy, and detector efficiency.
Data corrected for background, finite sample size,
dead time, source anisotropy, and detector efficiency.
